title: Procedural Storytelling in Game Design
creator: Tanya X. Short and Tarn Adams, editors
description: A multi-author practical collection on generating characters, histories, worlds, plots, dialogue, and player stories through systems. Case studies expose both productive methods and common failures.
useful: The variety of contributors prevents procedural narrative from being reduced to one algorithm or one kind of game.
audience: Narrative designers and programmers building stories that respond to systems and player action.
learning: Choose what parts of a story system should generate
Design data and rules that support meaningful variation
Recognize coherence and authorship problems in procedural narrative
